brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is time-series econometric forecasting: a scan statistic that detects an unknown-start/unknown-duration interval of forecast outperformance, plus gradient-boosted-tree regression on a high-dimensional encoding of the price-change distribution and an adaptive forecast-combination rule. Paper B's machinery is a neuroimaging group comparison that maps gray-matter regions with higher/lower structural connectivity in dementia versus healthy brains. There is no shared governing process or role-for-role correspondence—the overlap is only the generic fact that both analyze data statistically—so they do not share an underlying method.
